mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE jumps >0.3pp within a month.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and housing starts signals sectoral imbalance and impending demand contraction. When INDPRO rises while HOUST falls simultaneously, equity valuations contract due to rising recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power if the joint INDPRO/HOUST divergence fails to produce negative 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Within a monetary tightening cycle, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples. When FEDFUNDS is actively increasing, the S&P 500 experiences negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon as valuation multiples contract and earnings growth slows.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive FEDFUNDS hiking periods over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding commercial and industrial credit availability directly fuels corporate investment and consumer spending, boosting near-term earnings expectations. Rising RSAFS levels consistently generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if RSAFS increases are followed by negative 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 50% of observations across a rolling 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial output exceeding 1.5 percent over two months indicates supply-side disruption or demand collapse, forcing immediate equity repricing. This threshold breach produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in over 65% of cases following a >1.5% INDPRO drop over a two-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate elevation confirm a broadening labor market deterioration. This joint deterioration forces downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails if the combined PAYEMS/UNRATE deterioration does not produce negative 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, real GDP growth deceleration signals peak demand and impending margin compression. When GDPC1 growth slows below its 4-quarter moving average, equity risk premiums expand immediately, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward SPX returns remain positive or statistically neutral when GDPC1 growth decelerates below its 4-quarter MA during confirmed late-cycle periods over a 3-year window.
